notes: 76 total tests; 67 pytest + 19 bats; all pytest pass
With-skill achieves 100% assertion pass rate vs 78% baseline — a +22pp improvement
The skill adds ~4.5 minutes and ~48K tokens per run vs baseline
Key differentiators: testing strategy docs, context7 usage, bats-core selection for shell, unified test runner
Baseline is strong — competent test generation without workflow guidance, even finds real bugs
context7-used assertion may be non-discriminating: with-skill attempted but was denied by hooks; baseline naturally doesn't use it
testing-strategy-doc is the most discriminating assertion — with-skill always creates one, baseline never does
Shell-targeted baseline chose pure-bash framework over bats-core — valid but non-standard choice
